hint: Instructions written for a computer to execute.
Information processed or stored by a computer.
A unit of digital data, typically consisting of eight bits.
A magnetic storage medium for computer data.
A hand-held pointing device used with a computer.
The smallest unit of a digital image or display.
Malicious software designed to cause harm or spread.
Servers accessed over the internet for storage and computing.
Wireless networking technology that uses radio waves.
A device that emits a narrow, intense beam of light.
A small piece of semiconductor material carrying an integrated circuit.
The standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.
Irrelevant or inappropriate messages sent on the internet.
A hardware or software component that stores data so future requests can be served faster.
A computer or system that provides resources, data, or services to other computers.
A portable, relatively small computer suitable for use while traveling.
A person who uses computers to gain unauthorized access to data.
A device that forwards data packets between computer networks.
A distinct subset of the Internet with addresses sharing a common suffix.
A wireless, portable personal computer with a touchscreen interface.
A software patch or new version to improve a program.
A group of two or more computer systems linked together.
A software application for accessing information on the World Wide Web.
A small piece of data sent from a website and stored on the user's computer.
Software that is specifically designed to disrupt, damage, or gain unauthorized access.
Not physically existing as such but made by software to appear to do so.
A container consisting of one or more cells, converting chemical energy into electricity.
An output device that displays information in pictorial or text form.
A network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic.
The programs and other operating information used by a computer.
A structured set of data held in a computer.
A process or set of rules to be followed in calculations or other problem-solving operations.
The electronic circuitry within a computer that executes instructions.
The process of converting information or data into a code, especially to prevent unauthorized access.
The maximum rate of data transfer across a given path.
A short-range wireless technology standard for exchanging data.
A large high-speed computer, especially one supporting numerous peripherals or users.
A printed circuit board containing the principal components of a computer.
A mobile phone that performs many of the functions of a computer.
The number of pixels contained on a display monitor.
The science of communications and automatic control systems in both machines and living things.
Made or produced by human beings rather than occurring naturally (often paired with intelligence).
The art of writing or solving codes.
A program or piece of software designed to fulfill a particular purpose.
